entries.name: root-agent
entries.description: Orchestration layer that coordinates specialized subagents for security assessments

entries.name: source-aware-whitebox
entries.description: Coordination playbook for source-aware white-box testing with static triage and dynamic validation

entries.name: source-aware-sast
entries.description: Practical source-aware SAST and AST playbook for semgrep, ast-grep, gitleaks, and trivy fs

entries.name: fastapi
entries.description: Security testing playbook for FastAPI applications covering ASGI, dependency injection, and API vulnerabilities

entries.name: nestjs
entries.description: Security testing playbook for NestJS applications covering guards, pipes, decorators, module boundaries, and multi-transport auth

entries.name: nextjs
entries.description: Security testing playbook for Next.js covering App Router, Server Actions, RSC, and Edge runtime vulnerabilities

entries.name: graphql
entries.description: GraphQL security testing covering introspection, resolver injection, batching attacks, and authorization bypass

entries.name: deep
entries.description: Exhaustive security assessment with maximum coverage, depth, and vulnerability chaining

entries.name: quick
entries.description: Time-boxed rapid assessment targeting high-impact vulnerabilities

entries.name: standard
entries.description: Balanced security assessment with systematic methodology and full attack surface coverage

entries.name: firebase-firestore
entries.description: Firebase/Firestore security testing covering security rules, Cloud Functions, and client-side trust issues

entries.name: supabase
entries.description: Supabase security testing covering Row Level Security, PostgREST, Edge Functions, and service key exposure

entries.name: ffuf
entries.description: ffuf fuzzing syntax with matcher/filter strategy and non-interactive defaults.

entries.name: httpx
entries.description: ProjectDiscovery httpx probing syntax, exact probe flags, and automation-safe output patterns.

entries.name: katana
entries.description: Katana crawler syntax, depth/js/known-files behavior, and stable concurrency controls.

entries.name: naabu
entries.description: Naabu port-scanning syntax with host input, scan-type, verification, and rate controls.

entries.name: nmap
entries.description: Canonical Nmap CLI syntax, two-pass scanning workflow, and sandbox-safe bounded scan patterns.

entries.name: nuclei
entries.description: Exact Nuclei command structure, template selection, and bounded high-throughput execution controls.

entries.name: semgrep
entries.description: Exact Semgrep CLI structure, metrics-off scanning, scoped ruleset selection, and automation-safe output patterns.

entries.name: sqlmap
entries.description: sqlmap target syntax, non-interactive execution, and common validation/enumeration workflows.

entries.name: subfinder
entries.description: Subfinder passive subdomain enumeration syntax, source controls, and pipeline-ready output patterns.

entries.name: authentication-jwt
entries.description: JWT and OIDC security testing covering token forgery, algorithm confusion, and claim manipulation

entries.name: broken-function-level-authorization
entries.description: BFLA testing for action-level authorization failures across endpoints, admin functions, and API operations

entries.name: business-logic
entries.description: Business logic testing for workflow bypass, state manipulation, and domain invariant violations

entries.name: csrf
entries.description: CSRF testing covering token bypass, SameSite cookies, CORS misconfigurations, and state-changing request abuse

entries.name: idor
entries.description: IDOR/BOLA testing for object-level authorization failures and cross-account data access

entries.name: information-disclosure
entries.description: Information disclosure testing covering error messages, debug endpoints, metadata leakage, and source exposure

entries.name: insecure-file-uploads
entries.description: File upload security testing covering extension bypass, content-type manipulation, and path traversal

entries.name: mass-assignment
entries.description: Mass assignment testing for unauthorized field binding and privilege escalation via API parameters

entries.name: open-redirect
entries.description: Open redirect testing for phishing pivots, OAuth token theft, and allowlist bypass

entries.name: path-traversal-lfi-rfi
entries.description: Path traversal and file inclusion testing for local/remote file access and code execution

entries.name: race-conditions
entries.description: Race condition testing for TOCTOU bugs, double-spend, and concurrent state manipulation

entries.name: rce
entries.description: RCE testing covering command injection, deserialization, template injection, and code evaluation

entries.name: sql-injection
entries.description: SQL injection testing covering union, blind, error-based, and ORM bypass techniques

entries.name: ssrf
entries.description: SSRF testing for cloud metadata access, internal service discovery, and protocol smuggling

entries.name: subdomain-takeover
entries.description: Subdomain takeover testing for dangling DNS records and unclaimed cloud resources

entries.name: xss
entries.description: XSS testing covering reflected, stored, and DOM-based vectors with CSP bypass techniques

entries.name: xxe
entries.description: XXE testing for external entity injection, file disclosure, and SSRF via XML parsers

entries.name: orchestrator-worker-pattern
entries.description: Hierarchical agent pattern — a root orchestrator decomposes work and spawns specialized workers with focused context

entries.name: sandboxed-tool-runtime
entries.description: Agent tool execution should happen inside a disposable container, not the host — isolation by default, not opt-in

entries.name: scan-mode-as-skill
entries.description: Operational modes (quick/standard/deep) should be first-class skills, not hard-coded branching — each mode defines its own phases, priorities, and completion criteria

entries.name: shared-wiki-memory-pattern
entries.description: Multi-agent systems need a shared, append-only memory — one canonical note per entity, updated by every agent that touches it

entries.name: skill-injection-pattern
entries.description: Load up to N specialized skills into an agent at spawn time — dynamically tailor expertise per subtask
